programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A harassed international singing star is stalked by a sinister character, giving Dr Sloan the opportunity to unravel a mystery. Medical drama, starring Dick Van Dyke and Scott Baio

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A visitor dies in Dr Sloan's house, but the body disappears before the police arrive, raising fears that the medic imagined it all. Drama, starring Dick Van Dyke and Scott Baio

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Illusionist Eddie Clark gives Mark free tickets to his final show in the Magic Mansion before he and his partner Paul Madison go their separate ways. However, Paul plans to lay claim to all of their tricks when he goes solo, before an escapology stunt goes wrong and he dies on stage. Mark is unwilling to believe that Eddie could be guilty of murder, and decides to investigate. Guest starring Emma Samms

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Sylvester wins big on a game show, and the team must infiltrate a high-end car smuggling operation that is preparing to ship weapons to South America

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Toby discovers the supply at the LA blood bank has been tampered with, so the team tracks down the perpetrator in time to save a girl in desperate need of a heart transplant

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy lieutenant is found dead in the Pentagon's custodial closet, and surveillance footage indicates she was killed by a co-worker. However, Ziva and the team discover her supposed assailant actually died of a heart attack days before the crime was committed. Meanwhile, suspicions are raised regarding the eyeball left in Tony's drink, and the investigators' attention is fixed on the port-to-port murderer, who appears to still be at large

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony's one-time Baltimore PD partner Danny Price is murdered in a manner that suggests the port-to-port killer has struck again. However, a key difference in the way the victim was bound suggests the crime was actually the work of a copycat, forcing the investigators to consider whether Price had any enemies

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: New evidence reveals the serial killer has infiltrated the agency and is targeting a member of the NCIS team, prompting Gibbs to seek the guidance of his former mentor Mike Franks. A lead from Barrett's squad reveals the CIA has also been tracking the murderer - and its findings suggest the culprit could be an even deadlier foe than anticipated

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team attend the funeral of a fallen colleague before resuming their hunt for the man they believe to be the port-to-port killer. Vance frantically researches the suspect's background to learn more about his motivations and discover who his next target could be - but one of the investigators follows a clue that leads to danger

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony tries to piece together the events that led to him lying wounded in a hospital bed, having been shot in the chest through his bulletproof vest. Under the guidance of therapist Dr Rachel Cranston, his memory begins to return and suspicion falls on his ex-lover EJ Barrett. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Sarah Jane Morris

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Sylvester wins big on a game show, and the team must infiltrate a high-end car smuggling operation that is preparing to ship weapons to South America

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony and Ziva visit Paris to escort Nora Williams, the key witness in an embezzlement case, back to America - but on the return flight they realise an assassin has boarded the plane with them, determined to ensure she never reaches home soil to testify. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony and Ziva track a suspected terrorist to a warehouse, where they find traces of radioactive metal - together with evidence suggesting a nuclear attack is being planned on Washington, DC. Unfortunately, news of the threat leaks out to the public, triggering widespread panic. Naval crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Iraq war veteran Damon Werth asks Gibbs and the team to investigate the death of another former marine, who was murdered while working as a lorry driver, so Ziva hits the road to catch the killer. Joe Spano and Paul Telfer guest star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: When a Navy captain is killed in a robbery that went badly wrong, Gibbs' former mother-in-law surfaces as a witness in the investigation, and it transpires the victim had ties to a drug cartel that murdered the agent's first wife Shannon and daughter Kelly. Guest starring Gena Rowlands

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine is found with a gunshot wound six years after disappearing in Afghanistan, and the team discovers he vanished while carrying out an undercover operation. Gibbs suspects the wife could be hiding valuable information, but when a second woman challenges the soldier's identity and he subsequently dies in hospital, the team uncovers what really happened on that fateful mission

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gibbs joins forces with the FBI when the body of a congressman and former agent washes ashore in New Orleans. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo and Pauley Perrette

Meanwhile, Gibbs and Special Agent Pride chase leads in New Orleans after evidence points to a copycat killer. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Pauley Perrette and Scott Bakula

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee's girlfriend Delilah makes a breakthrough in a case involving the alleged death of a US navy lieutentant. However, when her boss orders her to back away, she turns to the NCIS team for help. Meanwhile, McGee talks to DiNozzo about whether he should move forward with his relationship. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team must ascertain whether the murder of a Navy officer, whose body was found on a rural road, was the result of a bar fight or because he knew too much about an attack on a fellow female officer. Meanwhile, DiNozzo and McGee begin a juice detox to purge their bodies of impurities. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the disappearance of photographer Martin Roe, who was due to testify at an army first lieutenant's court-martial after he captured a picture of what appeared to be a soldier killing an unarmed civilian. However, the photograph is leaked, and Roe is later found dead with a needle in his arm

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine gunnery sergeant Wendell Dobbs is gunned down in a drive-by shooting by assailants wielding AK-47s, and the team suspects the victim may have been involved in illegally importing automatic weapons into the country and supplying them to local gangs. Sam goes undercover to find the truth after learning Dobbs gave up his combat post to mentor his younger brother, who had been hanging out with a gang. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine Daniel Zuna is found dead in a pool of his own blood in an alleyway outside a night club, and the team's first stop is to investigate the mixed martial arts gym where the victim spent most of his off-duty time. Kensi poses as Zuna's new girlfriend and suspects the murder could be related to something that happened in Iraq, prompting Sam to go undercover as an MMA fighter in order to see if he can find out the truth. Crime drama, starring LL Cool J and Daniela Ruah

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A couple are taking in the view from a cliff top when a car driven by an already bleeding man crashes into theirs and teeters on the brink before plunging over the precipice. Callen and the team are brought in to investigate when the driver is identified as Lieutenant Brian Roth, and they discover that the car is registered to a Hollywood socialite Aubrey Darva. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Eric pulls the team together when he finds a video of kidnapped agent Dominic Vail posted on a jihadist website, with the terrorists holding him stating that he will be executed within 24 hours unless a comrade of theirs being held by the US government is released. One of the captors is identified as a being an associate of Sam, and his involvement also seems to indicate Dom is being held in Sudan. Crime drama, starring LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: An attack on a private plane immediately after it lands turns out to be the hijack of the secret army transfer of terrorist leader Ala A Din Keshwar, the man whose release was demanded in exchange for Dom's life. programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: The annual St Peter's Fair gets under way in Shrewsbury but a gruesome murder dampens the festivities. Antagonism between local merchants and visiting traders becomes increasingly ugly, and as civil unrest rages in the streets, Beringar is ordered to kill his friend Cadfael, whose investigations are causing King Stephen acute political embarrassment. Medieval whodunit, starring Derek Jacobi, Eoin McCarthy and Michael Culver

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Tom Beresford returns to Skelthwaite 20 years after a bitter family row, lured by the offer of a managerial position at Goddard's, and it's not long before he and his good-looking son are setting tongues wagging. Karen breaks all the rules to help two families come to terms with the consequences of a transplant operation, but her ill-advised actions backfire. Vic decides the time has come for a younger man to captain the Scorpions and an emotional Simon proposes to Anna. Drama, starring Tony Haygarth, Lesley Dunlop and Leslie Ash

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Vic makes a shock announcement about his future, Tom proves his worth in a crunch match for the Scorpions and Karen and Beth help reunite an estranged family after a baby girl goes missing. Drama, starring Leslie Ash and Kerrie Taylor

programme.title: Sharpe

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: Ally's dodgy dealings take a turn for the worse, and Barry has difficulty controlling Hazel who seems to be suffering from an overdose of sun, sea and sangria - to say nothing of gambling fever. Acclaimed comedy drama, starring Bill Paterson, Timothy Spall and Melanie Hill

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: Barry finally announces his long-awaited wedding to Hazel is definitely going ahead and Oz secretly embarks on a romance of his own. Needless to say, Ally is concocting another dodgy scheme in the background and, as he puts it into operation, the lads sail off over the horizon. Comedy drama, starring Jimmy Nail, Timothy Spall and Tim Healy

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo are hired to create a garden as a wedding present, and find a baby under a gooseberry bush. The discovery brings out Rosemary's maternal side as the pair investigate who abandoned the child. Their task is complicated by the murder of an artistic neighbour - and the two mysterious incidents appear to be connected. Starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo visit a resort in Spain to create a trophy garden for a pro-am tennis tournament run by a former champion. When one of the competitors is found dead, everyone assumes it to be a gruesome accident - but there is more to the case than meets the eye. While investigating, Laura enters the mysterious world of bullfighting, and Rosemary rediscovers her love of motor scooters

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The green-fingered amateur detectives work on a garden for an open-air theatre, where Rosemary's cousin is due to perform. However, the production suffers a setback when an actor shoots himself during rehearsal - and the gun is found to have contained live bullets instead of blanks. A member of the cast gets an attack of stage fright, and Laura is obliged to take her place in the play as the duo try to find the culprit. Drama, with Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: The owner and curator of Brokenwood's Historic Village, Charity Highmore-Browne, dies after being struck by an arrow. Mike and his team delve into the realm of New Zealand's colonial past as they deal with suspects including a blacksmith, a missionary, the local postmaster and the victim's sister. Neill Rea stars

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A murder is committed at the local army barracks using a legendary broken sword, and the incident leads the priest to investigate another death at Dunkirk 13 years previously. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: DI Max Harper believes he has enough evidence to connect the murders of Rosie Turner and Daniel Pembrook. Chief Inspector Briggs is shocked to discover the extent of Sergeant Fenton's deception, and even though he reveals Lenny Powell's connection to the brothel, it does not appear to be enough to save him. Starring Ben Turner

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A Scottish writer who has made a name for himself in Hollywood receives a death threat, propelling the grumpy Glaswegian detective into a murky world of jealousy and deceit. Mark McManus stars in the mystery, with James Macpherson, Blythe Duff, Ian Anders and John McGlynn

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: Four-part adaptation of Charlotte Bronte's novel about an orphan girl who struggles to survive the harsh treatment of a boarding school to embark on a career as a governess. Jane takes up her first position at the home of the alluring and unpredictable Edward Rochester, under the watchful eye of laundrywoman Grace Poole. Ruth Wilson, Toby Stephens and Francesca Annis star

programme.title: The Last Post
programme.desc: Drama about military police and their families faced with political and personal conflicts in a 1960s British colony on the Red Sea coast. An officer and his wife arrive in Aden, where he is to replace a much-loved captain. He soon has to adjust to life on the frontline, along with resentment among the men that he was appointed instead of the already-present Lt Ed Laithwaite. Honor is taken under the wing of Laithwaite's wife, Alison, who had been having an affair with Captain Page. Starring Jessie Buckley and Jeremy Neumark Jones

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Disgraced detective Zain Nadir is interviewed about drug trafficking in Hayes End Prison, where he is serving 10 years for perverting the course of justice, and agrees to help his colleagues infiltrate the gang involved. Jack is not sure whether he can trust his mole and fears the worst when a riot breaks out just as the police move in to arrest the operation's kingpin, Zain's cellmate Jason Forbes, who makes good his escape in the confusion. TJ Ramini and Lloyd McGuire guest star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Zain gets a frosty reception at the station when Heaton secures his release from prison to help with the recapture of escaped killer Jason Forbes, and Will can't help taking a swing at the man who callously disposed of Honey Harman's body in a bid to save his own skin. The pursuit leads to a gunpoint showdown, giving Zain a clear choice between escape and doing the right thing. TJ Ramini guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part story, new recruit DS Max Carter (Christopher Fox) joins the Sun Hill team from CO19 to investigate a tip-off about an armed robbery, only to find partner Terry drowning his sorrows after the informant is killed for his pains. Sally and Ben arrest two girls for failing to pay their cab fare, and a catastrophic train of events is unleashed when it emerges they were carrying a deadly cargo smuggled in from Ghana - and their boyfriends want it back

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Robert tries to arrange a love-match between Eleanor and one of his old comrades-in-arms, but meets with a conspicuous lack of success. Then a belligerent boxer with head injuries gives him an unexpected helping hand, and medicine starts taking a back seat to romance at the Thrift. Period drama, starring Jemma Redgrave, David Calder and Nicholas Farrell

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Eleanor unwittingly saves the life of a brutal East End villain, who begins terrorising patients and staff alike. Period drama, starring Jemma Redgrave

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Period medical drama chronicling the personal and professional consequences of Eleanor Bramwell's determined quest to become the first female surgeon at the East London Hospital. Jemma Redgrave stars

programme.desc: Feature-length adaptation of the best-selling novel, telling the tale of a 16-year-old struggling to keep her five brothers and sisters out of the workhouse with the help of a kindly carpenter. Starring James Fox, Tracy Whitwell and Edward Rawle-Hicks

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A water shortage means that the maternity home is busier than ever and two old schoolmates are reunited, but Marion shuns Shirley's attempts at renewing their friendship until they are brought together later in an unexpected way. Elsewhere, Barbara makes a revolutionary suggestion to help Sister Mary Cynthia treat the bedsores of her patient Tommy Mills, whose wife Gert may be more in need of care than he is. Fred thinks about taking the next step in his relationship with Violet. Drama, guest starring Una Stubbs and David Ryall

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Trixie and Sister Mary Cynthia team up to support a deaf mother-to-be, while another patient causes great concern when a case of morning sickness proves more serious than initially suspected. On the personal front, handyman Fred's daughter is less than delighted to hear about his forthcoming wedding. Will he be forced to choose between her and Violet?

programme.desc: Part one of two. Commander Adam Dalgleish investigates the case of a suspected serial killer and encounters greed and corruption, while also falling in love for the first time since the death of his wife. Thriller based on PD James's book, starring Martin Shaw, with Robert Hardy, Janie Dee and Jesse Spencer

Her first case involves a professional footballer who is forced to admit he is gay after his closet lover is run over in a hit-and-run incident involving his fiancee. Suspicion falls on the player's manager Ernie Rosen, who also happens to be his prospective father-in-law and anxious to avoid the shame of a scandal in the family, but Sally uncovers evidence to suggest he is covering for someone even closer to home

programme.desc: Mr Humphries catches a rare and contagious tropical disease - and it is not long before his workmates are getting on each other's nerves even more than usual while quarantined from the outside world. Vintage comedy, starring Mollie Sugden, John Inman and Frank Thornton

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The trio decide there is profit to be made from a heatwave, so Seymour designs a vendor's side car cooler to put Compo into business as an ice-cream man. Comedy, starring Michael Aldridge and Bill Owen

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo and Clegg hatch a devious plan to free henpecked Wally and Howard from their wives - if only for a few hours. Bill Owen and Peter Sallis star

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: A prostitute turns up at the station claiming to have been raped, and as Gently looks back at past cases, he is shocked to discover that very few have been successfully prosecuted - if they made it to court in the first place. Risking animosity from his colleagues, he becomes suspicious when he notices the same police inspector's name appearing repeatedly in the files. To complicate matters, the body of another rape victim is found, prompting a visit to the local brothel. Meanwhile, Bacchus has started an affair with Gemma, the wife of a fellow copper. Martin Shaw, Lee Ingleby and Lisa McGrillis star, with guest appearances from Denise Welch, Derek Riddell and Emily Woof

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: In a special edition of the detective drama that features only two members of the team, Gerry Standing and Steve McAndrew travel to Glasgow, where a new Ucos section is being set up. While there, they agree to assist the investigation into the 1993 unsolved murder of James Soutar, the wealthy owner of a string of betting shops. Cathy Sinclair, one of the beneficiaries of the bookie's will at his death, had a care-home upbringing in common with Soutar, but she claims to have no idea who he was or why she was left £15,000. McAndrew has a personal stake in the case - the original investigating officer was corrupt cop Frank McNair, who had an affair with his wife, and their paths soon cross. The London-based officers' questions begin to unsettle someone and a campaign of victimisation against them begins, but it's a revelation from McAndrew's girlfriend Charley that reawakens a long-dormant scandal and clears the path to a solution. Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson star

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Leo and Harry are called to investigate the unexpected deaths of three patients on the same ward at a London hospital, and are introduced to DC Julia Catina and DI Frank Skipper. Suspicion falls on a professor, but when he commits suicide, Nikki comes under pressure from his daughter to find a reason for his actions, and the pathologist soon makes a link to a case involving rape and murder

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Penny makes a choice when Hugh and Toke turn up the romantic pressure. When Charlie is unfairly sacked by the Headmaster, Meryl resorts to blackmail to get her reinstated

programme.title: Mercy Street

programme.title: Casualty

When a patient she knows dies, her sadness is tempered by the opportunity the situation presents to obtain the drugs she needs. Connie lashes out in her frustration over Jacob's new relationship, and a friendly bet between Rosa and Charlie soon gets out of control

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Denton fights for her life as disturbing details emerge of a conspiracy involving corrupt officers, and Arnott uncovers startling new details about the detective's personal life which appear to support her claim that she is being framed. Meanwhile, Hastings puts his career on the line to pursue a high-level target. Crime thriller, starring Adrian Dunbar and Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Aston Villa v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Villa Park and Brighton & Hove Albion v Leicester City at Amex Stadium. The Villans were looking to do the double over their Midlands rivals after prevailing 1-0 at Molineux in December, while Leicester were hoping to boost their top four hopes with a third win of the season over the Seagulls, having defeated them at home in the league and FA Cup. Plus, Burnley v Arsenal and Sheffield United v Southampton

programme.title: Margin Call (2011)
programme.desc: A bitter executive is dismissed from an investment bank, but plans his revenge by handing secret information on the company's fortunes to a junior analyst. When the information is decoded, it reveals that the company is on the brink of a devastating financial crisis, a revelation that is soon spreading throughout the workforce. Drama, starring Kevin Spacey, Paul Bettany and Jeremy Irons

Plus, Burnley v Arsenal and Sheffield United v Southampton

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: Andrew Marr's guests are education secretary Gavin Williamson MP, shadow health secretary Jonathan Ashworth MP and Dr Susan Hopkins of Public Health England. The news review is with BBC Scotland editor Sarah Smith, and money saving expert and journalist Martin Lewis

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents moral and religious discussions on topical issues, with contributions by members of the studio audience

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: Rev Kate Bottley introduces a church service for the season of Lent. The service comes from All Saints Church in Hale Barns, led by the Rev Clair Jaquiss

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Catherine Southon help two teams look for items at the Kent County Showground in Detling, while host Charlie Ross Charlie finds out about explorer Julius Brenchley

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins reflects on personal prayer with young Christians in East Sussex, while Gemma Hunt hears how prayer changed the life of the founder of 24-7 Prayer

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore and her team find four tip-bound treasures found in Greater Manchester and the West Midlands, and transform them into profitable pieces

programme.title: Planes: Fire & Rescue (2014)
programme.desc: A racing plane suffers damage to his engine, and realises he can never compete again. He starts a new career as a member of a firefighting squadron, which proves even more dangerous than he expected when the aircraft have to deal with a massive forest blaze. Animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Dane Cook and Ed Harris

programme.title: Attenborough's Life in Colour
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the many ways animals use colour, with cameras built specifically for this series revealing a world normally invisible to human eyes. The peacock's tail has evolved entirely to impress females and is one of the most extreme colour ornaments in the natural world, while mandrill baboons signal their strength and status with striking red and blue face masks, the brighter the colours, the higher ranking the male

programme.title: A Service of Celebration for Commonwealth Day
programme.desc: To mark Commonwealth Day, the Queen shares her annual message. Other members of the Royal Family, including the Prince of Wales, the Duchess of Cornwall, the Duke and Duchess of Cambridge and the Countess of Wessex, join the celebrations and speak about why our Commonwealth links are so important. Presented by Anita Rani from Westminster Abbey, the celebration hears voices from across the world alongside music and readings, including performances by Nitin Sawhney, Lianne La Havas, Alexis Ffrench and a reflection by double Commonwealth gold medal-winning athlete Denise Lewis

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Charlotte Smith visits the Colne Valley Regional Park, a landscape of forest, fields and waterways on the edge of west London. She does battle with an invasive species, gets up close with tiny creatures that show the health of the park's rivers, meets a savvy septuagenarian saving her farm from development, and helps secure a future for one of our most endangered mammals, the water vole. Tom Heap asks whether eco-education should be at the heart of our school system, Adam Henson introduces new boar George II to his sows, and wildlife cameraman Jack Perks gets up close with otters

programme.title: Attenborough's Life in Colour
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the extraordinary ways that some animals use colour to hide and disappear into the background. The Bengal tigers of central India use their striped patterns to dazzle colour blind prey, while zebras on the Masai Mara use motion dazzle to confuse predators. Other animals use colour to trick and to deceive. On Australia's Great Barrier Reef, a blue-striped blenny uses colours to mimic other fish and launch a sudden attack

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Kenilworth Castle, near Coventry. Intriguing treasures brought in for valuation include a miniature safe, one of the world's first interactive golf games, and a trilby belonging to an 1980s pop star. Fiona also delves into the story of a car industry entrepreneur who saved the estate from ruin

programme.title: Bloodlands
programme.desc: Tom and Niamh launch a manhunt for Adam Corry, who has disappeared in mysterious circumstances. Tori Matthews agrees to help Tom in the quest to identify the notorious assassin Goliath, who appears to be at large once again. As pressure builds on the police, Tom must confront the possibility Goliath may be one of his fellow officers

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Manchester City v Manchester United at Old Trafford and Liverpool v Fulham at Anfield. City are the title favourites, but were held to a goalless draw by their neighbours in the reverse fixture and United defeated them 2-1 here last term. Elsewhere, struggling Fulham were hoping to capitalise on the Reds' recent poor home form, having already held them to a draw at Craven Cottage this season. Plus, Tottenham Hotspur v Crystal Palace and West Bromwich Albion v Newcastle United

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Reshmin Chowdhury presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including West Ham United v Chelsea, Manchester City v Everton and Manchester United v Aston Villa

programme.title: Why Is Covid Killing People of Colour?
programme.desc: David Harewood sets out to investigate the high Covid-19 death rates in black and minority ethnic patients in the UK, and what they reveal about health inequality in modern Britain. Visiting health experts and doctors at hospitals in areas with high rates of admissions, he discovers the impact of job opportunities and air pollution in deprived communities can leave the residents far more vulnerable, as well as hearing from patients who feel their concerns were disregarded by health professionals

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym and Gethin are joined by Dr Xand, who is on hand with his medical advice and support. Gloria Hunniford is supporting the Stroke Association and tells us how to spot the signs of a stroke. Naga Munchetty talks about her new Panorama investigation into racism, and we launch a brand new dance series for Red Nose Day

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin explore properties about to be auctioned in Upstreet in Kent, Yardley in Birmingham, and Constantine in Cornwall

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley present appeals for help with unsolved cases. Reporter John-Paul Davies is out and about with stories from across the UK

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Officers go undercover to identify shops illegally selling knives to children, and they unmask a car parking scam where vehicles are being left in muddy fields

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles presents the show from Wrexham, North Wales, with experts Caroline Hawley and Danny Sebastian, and heads to Chester Cathedral to learn how spiders inspired artists

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel tries to stop a dangerous hunger strike, Ruhma offers an ear as pressure builds on Luca, and Zara worries for a grieving friend who believes in communing with the dead

programme.title: Moving On
programme.desc: Five dramas linked by the central theme of moving on. A man is stunned when his father, whom he thought was dead, turns up at his wedding

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a young canine-loving couple from Hertfordshire, who are hoping for a fresh start in a new High Weald home, and have a budget of £600,000

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Akis Petretzikis and Anna Haugh prepare meals in 20 minutes, using ingredients chosen by two best friends, one of whom likes traditional cooking while the other is more adventurous

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers are intrigued by a vintage petrol lighter based on Donald Campbell's land speed record racing car, a Georgian needlework box, a 1970s kitsch musical drinks decanter and a collection of Second World War memorabilia. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty travels across the country to understand what race and racism mean in the UK, in the wake of the international conversation triggered by the killing of George Floyd

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Denise confronts Chelsea after Jack tells her about the blackmail - but she is surprised by her daughter's reaction. Kim plays matchmaker for Peter and Ash, and despite the latter's reluctance they decide to go on a date - only for Suki to dampen the mood. Martin is concerned when Ruby makes it clear she wants to move on, and a talent scout appears in Walford

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Previously unseen material from the comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Former champions Irini Tzortzoglou and Jane Devonshire judge today's efforts, as five more contestants prepare signature meals and dishes involving their favourite ingredients. After one of the five cooks is eliminated from the contest, the remaining four must prepare a two-course meal to win one of the two remaining places in the quarter final

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: RuPaul's search for the UK's Next Drag Race Superstar continues. This week, the queens face one of their toughest challenge yet as they are asked to write and perform their own comedy stand-up routines. Comedy legend Dawn French joins Michelle Visage and Alan Carr on the judging panel as another queen takes her leave…

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Line of Duty stars Vicky McClure and Adrian Dunbar talk about the hotly awaited sixth series of the BBC's crime drama. Star Wars' Daisy Ridley chats about starring in sci-fi adventure Chaos Walking, Breaking Bad's Bryan Cranston reflects on his role as a New Orleans judge in the TV drama Your Honor and Brit star Chiwetel Ejiofor promotes his romantic comedy heist, Locked Down. Plus, Imelda May performs Made to Love

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Ninjas, sumo, a life-size origami boat and costumes straight out of a manga comic are dreamt up by Japan-loving brother and sister Sunee and Taeya - and it's a race against time as they wait for a very special delivery to arrive all the way from Tokyo

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: A nine-year-old from Brentwood seeks a marble-run den in which he can pursue his ultimate dream of becoming a rollercoaster designer

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: The winners of the 2021 Blue Peter Book Awards are announced

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith presents the final of the contestant, which sees the three remaining contestants aiming to become top dog-groomer - and win themselves the Golden Stanley trophy to prove it. All they have to do is execute one of the toughest challenges in the industry - a continental clip on an elegant standard poodle. As if that were not enough, they have to guess a breed of dog while blindfolded, before one last dogwalk parade. Who will impress judges Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor the most?

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of the morning session on day two of the biennial event, held this year at Arena Torun in Poland. With commentary from Steve Cram, Steve Backley and Andrew Cotter

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Sylvia Syms revisits archive interviews with some of Britain's biggest stars of the 1960s, including Sean Connery, George Lazenby, Peter Sellers, Peter O'Toole and Julie Christie

programme.title: The Ipcress File (1965)
programme.desc: An intelligence agent investigating the kidnap and brainwashing of Britain's most renowned scientists unpicks a complex chain of connections and high-level intrigue, while detecting evidence of double-dealing and counter-espionage. Spy thriller based on Len Deighton's novel, starring Michael Caine, Sue Lloyd, Gordon Jackson and Nigel Green

programme.title: DNA Family Secrets
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley helps people answer life-changing questions by examining their DNA. In the first episode, she comes to the aid of Richard, who wants to learn if a man claiming to be his biological father is the genuine article, and Charlie, who wants to know what her chances are of contracting the same terminal illness as her father

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Master craftsman Simon Parfett and his team help people with a little land or a beautiful garden generate extra income by creating their own unique B&Bs. They begin by heading to the picturesque Wye Valley to help George and Al transform their battered old family camper van

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The designers work alone for the first time in the contest, each being assigned to transform a beach hut at Walton-on-the-Naze in Essex, in just two days on a budget of £1000. Working in a small space presents a serious challenge, and while some designers decide to incorporate existing features in their vision, others ruthlessly strip the huts bare in a bid to make their mark. Helping Michelle Ogundehin judge the results is journalist and interior designer Sophie Robinson

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of the evening session on day two of the biennial event, held this year at Arena Torun in Poland. British pole-vaulter Holly Bradshaw will be hoping to go one better than in 2019 and emulate her 2013 triumph this evening, and medals are also decided in the women's long jump, 1500m and 400m, and the men's 400m and 60m. Another Team GB medal hope in Elliot Giles is also expected to be in action in the semi-finals of the 800m. With commentary from Steve Cram, Steve Backley and Andrew Cotter

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Documentary about the change of musical direction in 1978, when pop singles were slowly ousted by disco, punk and new wave artists. The programme features contributions by members of the Boomtown Rats, Squeeze, Boney M and Sham 69 - among others - and archive performances by the likes of Elvis Costello, the Undertones, Ian Dury and the Blockheads and the Jam

programme.title: Dave Allen: The Immaculate Selection
programme.desc: A compilation of the finest, funniest and most irreverent routines by the Irish comedy writer and performer, known for his combination of laid-back storytelling and anger at social and political hypocrisy. The programme features a selection of BBC archive material originally recorded between 1971 and 1986

programme.title: Dave Allen Live: On Life
programme.desc: A selection of monologues by the Irish comedian, in which he reflects on the idiocies of life, meditating on everything from the process of ageing through to the invasive tendencies of the telephone

programme.title: Mindhorn (2016)
programme.desc: The star of a 1980s sci-fi cop show sets out to make it in Hollywood, but ends up with his life and career in ruins. Years later, he gets the chance to be a real hero when the police need his help. A dangerous fugitive is on the loose, who believes the character the actor played - a detective with a bionic eye that can detect lies - is real. Comedy, starring Julian Barratt, Essie Davis and Steve Coogan

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1978
programme.desc: A selection of classic songs from 1978, a year in which punk music and new wave rebellion co-existed with pop, disco, soul, reggae and prog rock. Featuring appearances on the show by Ian Dury, Elvis Costello, Blondie, Bob Marley, the Boomtown Rats, Kate Bush, Sham 69, Brotherhood of Man, Dan Hill, Boney M, 10cc and Manfred Mann's Earth Band

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin travels to Shropshire to meet the Shrewsbury Bloomers, who have been taking part in the annual floral competition for decades, and plant 300,000 flowers each year

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Horticulturist Christine Walkden visits the gardens of Staffordshire, taking to the water to remove some unwanted pests at Biddulph Grange, and trying out tree-hugging at Shugborough Hall. Plus, how the area's industrial heritage shaped the county's landscape

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani is taken on a tour of one of London's cemeteries, as she discovers a trove of treasures right on her doorstep and sets out to crack the mystery of a non-native bird. Adam Henson shows how a super-meal is specially concocted for his animals during the winter months, and Tom Heap investigates the controversial decision that allows UK farmers to use a banned pesticide on their fields

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway climbs the Nash Point lighthouse, a place he used to visit as a child. His coastal walk takes him along the 'Jurassic' Glamorgan coastline. On the way he goes fossil hunting, searches for an elusive chough and learns the story of a pregnancy-testing frog. He begins his walk at St Donat's Castle, once owned by newspaper magnate Sir Randolph Hearst, which became a party palace for the rich and famous, then heads for Dunraven Bay

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of the morning session on day three of the biennial event, held this year at Arena Torun in Poland. With commentary from Steve Cram, Steve Backley and Andrew Cotter

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt takes a look back at some of his favourite recipes and best moments from the programme

programme.title: Your Garden Made Perfect
programme.desc: Designers Manoj Malde and Tom Massey come up with alternative ideas for the West Sussex garden of wheelchair user Tracey, who can only venture into certain parts of her current space with assistance, and her husband Jake, who dreams of having a low-maintenance plot that he doesn't have to keep looking neat and tidy. Plus, Joel Bird helps a Liverpool couple turn their tiny, dull courtyard into a colourful paradise. Presented by Angela Scanlon

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of the afternoon session on day three of the biennial event, held this year at Arena Torun in Poland. Elliot Giles is a major British hope for this concluding session in the men's 800m, having set the second fastest indoor time in history last month. Medals are also awarded in the men's 60m hurdles, pole vault, heptathlon, 3,000m and 4x400m relay, and the women's 60m hurdles, triple jump, high jump, 800m, 60m and 4x400m relay. With commentary from Steve Backley, Steve Cram and Andrew Cotter

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and his team head to Somerset to work with Vicki and Bryan, who have a space-age geodome but have no idea how to transform it into a welcoming rental

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon reaches the United States, travelling down the Rocky Mountains from Montana to Colorado. In the wide-open prairies of Montana, the cowboy way of life is under threat from a long-term decline in the rural economy, but Simon meets a former Silicon Valley executive who has envisaged a new future for the land. In a country with the highest incarceration rate in the world, Simon's final destination on this leg is a Colorado town that depends almost entirely on 11 prisons. Simon witnesses how inmates are put to work for the state, and how virtual reality is being used to prepare prisoners for life on the outside

programme.title: Chris Packham's Animal Einsteins
programme.desc: The presenter introduces animals that build structures and adapt the environment for their own benefit, creating safe places to sleep and raise young or traps to catch prey. The programme examines how the huge dams built by beavers can completely transform the surrounding landscape, the function of the different types of silk spiders are capable of producing, and how leafcutter ants build underground gardens where they farm their own food

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan is joined on the topical comedy show by guests Roisin Conaty and Jamie Laing, as well as his mum and his hand-picked focus group of 20. Together they take a humorous look at the week's biggest talking points, from front page news to trending topics, helping the nation to navigate their way through these tricky times

programme.title: Funny Festival Live
programme.desc: Part of BBC Two's Festival of Funny, this brand new live stand-up series aims to spread a bit of joy and laughter by showcasing the very best comedy talent. Jason Manford kicks off the series by welcoming three brilliant stand-ups to the stage to share their highs and lows of lockdown as well as other hilarious observations. Judi Love shares her passion for masks, while Toussaint Douglass explains why he hates cats. Newcomer Michael Stranney gives us an insight into his unique family life, and Jayde Adams is still trying to get away with murder. Each episode is filmed at a Covid-secure comedy club, in front of the production team with a virtual audience tuning in from home.

programme.title: Felix Dexter in Douglas
programme.desc: An ill-fated barrister is on his way to meet his girlfriend's parents, when an encounter with a traveller changes his life. Comedy drama first aired in 1996, written by and starring Felix Dexter

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's Tour of Scotland
programme.desc: The comedian explores Glasgow, calling in at a martial arts club in the east end and exploring the ruins of Crookston Castle. Frankie's home city is no stranger to riots and protest and he meets one high-profile campaigner who became famous as one of the 'Glasgow Girls'. On the journey to his stand-up show at the Gaiety Theatre in Ayr, Frankie pops into the town of Troon to meet some members of one of the most famous showmen families in Scotland. Upon reaching Ayr, he catches up with fellow performer Elaine C Smith to discuss performing onstage and their different styles of comedy

programme.title: Festival of Funny: Shorts
programme.desc: A collection of distinctive comedy shorts from the next generation, showcasing creative talent from all across the UK and beyond

programme.title: In the Loop (2009)
programme.desc: A witless government minister makes a few ill-chosen remarks on the possibility of Britain and the US going to war in the Middle East, and suddenly finds himself out of his depth in international politics as pro and anti-war factions try to influence him and a fearsome spin doctor is sent to control the situation. Satirical comedy, starring Peter Capaldi, Tom Hollander and James Gandolfini

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Angela tries to restart the Company, but Noah, Peter and Matt are intent on resuming their normal lives. Claire starts college and must decide how much her past will affect her future, Hiro and Ando set up a 'Dial a Hero' firm back home in Japan, and Tracy targets Danko and his men. Meanwhile, a new threat arrives in the form of a mysterious carnival. Fantasy drama, starring Hayden Panettiere

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Hiro meets Samuel at the carnival 14 years in the past and cannot help but make a subtle change to history. Noah finds a clue relating to Danko's murder when Tracy calls him to the scene, before joining forces with Peter to learn more about the victim and his killer. Back at university, Claire and Gretchen refuse to believe the police's explanation of what happened to Annie

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week. The panellists are Culture Secretary Oliver Dowden, Shadow Housing Secretary Thangam Debbonaire, farmer and entrepreneur Wilfred Emmanuel-Jones, Executive Director of the Equality Trust Wanda Wyporska and Professor Robin Shattock, Head of Mucosal Infection and Immunity at Imperial College London

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Hanssen is thrown when Sahira's seriously ill father is admitted to Keller and he tries to avoid treating him. Cameron is backed into a corner when Bobby arrives on the ward threatening to talk. Ange and Dom clash over his decision to sue the hospital as she is forced to choose between her son and her morals

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste report on a woman suffering from what she felt was a type of Stockholm syndrome, in that she was unable to escape a relationship with a man who took tens of thousands of pounds from her

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don and the team look back at some of the floral highlights of 2020. It is a celebration of some of the star flowers of gardens, from tulips and roses to salvias and dahlias. There is also another chance to see what viewers were getting up to in their own gardens throughout the year

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: Alicia McCarthy presents a round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright demonstrates exercises designed to get muscles moving, and is joined by singer-songwriter and footballer Chelcee Grimes

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bitesize: 11-12 Year Olds
programme.desc: Reflections in maths, the Suffragettes, and Jodie Kidd reading Journey to the River Sea.

programme.title: Bitesize: 12-13 Year Olds
programme.desc: Sensory language, and Dina Asher-Smith reads from Noughts and Crosses by Malorie Blackman

programme.title: Bitesize: 13-14 Year Olds
programme.desc: The Suffragettes, enlargement of shapes, and writing techniques

programme.title: 1066: A Year to Conquer England
programme.desc: Docudrama in which Dan Snow explores the political intrigues and family betrayals between Vikings, Anglo-Saxons and Normans that led to the Battle of Hastings. The first edition reveals how the death of Edward the Confessor without an heir triggered a bitter race to succeed him as king of England, with Earl Harold successfully taking the crown. However, William of Normandy believes the throne has been promised to him and decides to invade, while Viking king Harald Hardrada also fancies himself as monarch

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Robshaw family experience life at the turn of the 20th century, and discover how the food people ate and the way they consumed it helped shape the modern family. An ordinary house in South London will be their time machine, transporting them through five decades and two world wars. Guided by presenters Giles Coren and social historian Polly Russell, they'll trace the changes to Britain's diet and the extraordinary social transformation they reveal. Monica Galetti and Chas and Dave make guest appearances

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Homeowners in York open their doors to five neighbours, featuring a Victorian cottage, a 1960s farmhouse given a new lease of life and a 1980s bungalow

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Wildlife documentary making use of scientific research into animal anatomy to explain how various species are uniquely built to thrive in specific habitats. Chris Packham begins by examine details the penguin, detailing how the aquatic, flightless bird's legs, wings and body shape have allowed it to conquer a wide range of landscapes, from deep forests and tropical waters to bustling cities and the toughest place on the planet - Antarctica

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A collection of interesting and previously unseen finds from the show, with visits to Margam Country Park in south Wales, Highcliffe Castle in Dorset and Powderham Castle in Devon

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Mina Anwar, Glen Moore, Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Shaun Williamson go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and his team head to the Black Mountains in south-east Wales to help Alex and Laura finish building a tiny house on wheels

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.desc: The food writer and former Great British Bake Off judge continues her celebration of the everyday food and ingredients she has always loved to cook, and which have formed the cornerstone of her work for more than six decades. In this edition, Mary shares recipes for inspiring hearty dishes full of wholesome goodness, from her secret tip for a rich bolognese sauce to a soup that will warm the cockles. She also dons a white suit and tries her hand at beekeeping, collecting fresh honey to make a winning pudding

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contestants to brave the black chair, asking them questions on the specialist subjects of the Russian Revolution, King Crimson, The Hunger Games, and the essays and journalism of George Orwell, before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell presents the first semi-final of the quiz as two teams compete to guess the connections between things that seem utterly random at first glance, with one set of clues asking what completes the sequence of Vijfhuizen: Houses, Sichuan: Rivers, and Tripoli: Cities

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Two teams of four students battle it out in the penultimate quarter-final, hoping to make it to the next stage of the competition as they try to win two matches and progress to the semi-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Miss World 1970: Beauty Queens and Bedlam
programme.desc: The story of the protest at the contest, when protestors dramatically disrupted the event and kickstarted a feminist revolution in front of an audience of millions. Hannah Berryman's film uses archive, animation and contributions from key players to offer an entertaining and thought-provoking celebration of a night of game-changing anarchy

programme.title: Funny Festival Live
programme.desc: Stand-up performances by established and up-and-coming comedians, recorded in front of a live virtual audience over three nights at a Covid-safe venue in south London

programme.title: Harry Hill's Lonely Island
programme.desc: Silent slapstick comedy short from Harry Hill

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous: The Movie (2016)
programme.desc: Worried about her finances when her book fails to find a publisher, Patsy is given the tip that Kate Moss has left her PR company and will be looking for new representation. Things start to go awry when the plot to approach the model goes horribly wrong and Patsy and Edina find themselves vilified by the entire nation and have to flee the country. Comedy based on the BBC sitcom, starring Jennifer Saunders, Joanna Lumley and Julia Sawalha

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani is taken on a tour of one of London's cemeteries, as she discovers a trove of treasures right on her doorstep and sets out to crack the mystery of a non-native bird. Adam Henson shows how a super-meal is specially concocted for his animals during the winter months, and Tom Heap investigates the controversial decision that allows UK farmers to use a banned pesticide on their fields

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: Mary Beard talks to cultural creatives who make other worlds for people's exploration and delight. Booker Prize-winner Marlon James discusses his Dark Star trilogy, which turns the fantasy genre on its head, and David Greig, artistic director of Edinburgh's Lyceum Theatre, takes viewers on a quest to an ancient woodland, where appealing to the spirits seems completely rational during a pandemic. Mary also chairs a debate on whether the occult is enjoying a revival or if it has always been more popular than she imagines

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: A man is murdered for no apparent reason by a guest at a party. Detectives use CCTV and financial transactions to track the suspect and arrest him just before he flees the country. The forensic team employ a mixture of traditional and digital forensics to try to determine whether or not the witnesses are telling the truth

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste return with more investigations into online dating scams, beginning with the exposure of an elaborate fraud

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Documentary charting the story of how film-makers used the Himalayan mountains for propaganda, as the world's great powers sent military-style expeditions to conquer the peaks between the 1920s and the 1960s. As a result, some of the range's most iconic mountaineering feats have been captured on film, including the preparations for Mallory and Irvine ill-fated ascent of Mount Everest in 1924, and the final conquest of the mountain by Hillary and Tenzing in 1953

programme.title: Himalaya with Michael Palin
programme.desc: Michael's journey takes him to Bhutan, where he enjoys a last glimpse of the Himalaya before heading to Paro to enjoy a Buddhist festival. Here he witnesses archery contests and the unfolding of a giant scroll during the night of a full moon

programme.title: Man in Room 301
programme.desc: Seppo travels to Greece, where Risto questions Niina about how well she knows Leo. In Finnish

programme.title: Man in Room 301
programme.desc: Olivia is frantic with worry when neither Anna nor Tytti return home from Sonia's party. In Greece, Kalle sneaks out of the hotel room to go climbing with Leo. In Finnish

programme.title: Vikings
programme.desc: Neil Oliver continues his attempt to shed new light on the Norse warriors by examining their sophisticated trading skills, and visits locations in Russia, Turkey and Ireland to explain how they established a business empire. He also explores the vast array of goods that were bought and sold by Viking merchants and settlers, including Chinese silks and Pictish slaves

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the edition first aired on September 20, with performances by artists including Snap!, the Charlatans, the Farm, MC Tunes versus 808 State, Steve Miller Band, the Stone Roses and Twenty 4 Seven featuring Captain Hollywood

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Anthea Turner hosts the edition first aired on September 27, with performances by artists including Londonbeat, the Wedding Present, Status Quo, Bass-O-Matic, Maria McKee, Monie Love featuring True Image and Depeche Mode

programme.title: Girls in Bands at the BBC
programme.desc: Compilation of performances by some of the best female musicians in rock, from the 1970s to the present day. Artists featured include American rock group Fanny, the powerful vocals of Elkie Brooks on Vinegar Joe's Proud to Be a Honky Tonk Woman, the poetry of Patti Smith's Horses and upbeat energy of the Go-Go's on We Got the Beat

programme.title: Himalaya with Michael Palin
programme.desc: Michael's journey takes him to Bhutan, where he enjoys a last glimpse of the Himalaya before heading to Paro to enjoy a Buddhist festival. Here he witnesses archery contests and the unfolding of a giant scroll during the night of a full moon

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Documentary charting the story of how film-makers used the Himalayan mountains for propaganda, as the world's great powers sent military-style expeditions to conquer the peaks between the 1920s and the 1960s. As a result, some of the range's most iconic mountaineering feats have been captured on film, including the preparations for Mallory and Irvine ill-fated ascent of Mount Everest in 1924, and the final conquest of the mountain by Hillary and Tenzing in 1953

programme.title: The Many Faces of
programme.desc: Profile of the Manchester-born comedian known for his deadpan humour and jokes at the expense of his wife and mother-in-law. The programme explores his beginnings in northern clubs and appearance on talent show Opportunity Knocks, and charts his rise to stardom. Contributors include John Cleese, Robert Webb and Russell Kane. Narrated by Sally Phillips

programme.title: Dan Snow on Lloyd George: My Great-Great-Grandfather
programme.desc: The presenter traces the story of his great-great grandfather David Lloyd George, who served as prime minister from 1916 to 1922 and played a key role in creating victory for the Allies during the First World War

programme.title: World of Stonehenge
programme.desc: Neil Oliver reports on a 5,000-year-old temple discovered in Orkney. Built 500 years before Stonehenge, the undisturbed artefacts found there, including wall decorations, pigments and paint pots, are helping to increase knowledge and understanding of the Neolithic people. Special effects are used to re-create the structure in 3D, allowing Neil to walk inside and explore it

programme.title: The Gangster, the Cop, the Devil (2019)
programme.desc: A gang boss comes close to being the latest victim of an elusive serial killer. Deciding that the murderer's presence on the streets is as much a threat to organised crime as to law-abiding citizens, he enters into an alliance with the detective on the case to end the killing spree. South Korean crime thriller, starring Ma Dong-seok and Mu-Yeol Kim. In Korean and English

programme.title: The Genius of Marie Curie - The Woman Who Lit Up the World
programme.desc: Polish physicist and chemist Marie Curie became a celebrity during her lifetime, attracting media attention for being the first woman to win a Nobel Prize. This docu-drama looks at the woman behind the science, revealing a tenacious mother who had to survive the pain of the loss of husband and collaborator Pierre and the public humiliation of a doomed love affair, but who also discovered two elements and coined the term radioactivity. David Malone narrates, with readings by Geraldine James

programme.title: Black Nurses: The Women Who Saved the NHS
programme.desc: The story of the thousands of Caribbean and African women who answered the call more than 70 years ago to come to the UK and help build the National Health Service. Told by the women themselves, the documentary reveals the price they paid in leaving behind their families, and examines their struggle to overcome racism and their fight for career progression, as well as their cultural impact here and abroad

programme.title: The Many Faces of
programme.desc: Profile of the Manchester-born comedian known for his deadpan humour and jokes at the expense of his wife and mother-in-law. programme.desc: The presenter traces the story of his great-great grandfather David Lloyd George, who served as prime minister from 1916 to 1922 and played a key role in creating victory for the Allies during the First World War

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's World of Steam, Steel and Stone
programme.desc: The steeplejack's fascination with mining, which led him to dig his own coal mine in his back garden. Fred was also keen to teach viewers about the history of the industry, educating people about the dangers faced by those working down the pit, and the skills workers required

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: A cabin finds a peaceful resting place in a warm setting of lush, lakeside greenery in this oval-shaped painting by Bob Ross

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and art expert Philip Mould travel to Australia to verify whether a painting bought from an English auction site is a lost work by impressionist Tom Roberts, one of the country's most important artists. The owners have fallen on hard times, and desperately need to prove the painting is genuine. The investigation leads them to retrace the time Roberts spent in Britain and uncovers some alarming forgeries

programme.title: The Story of Welsh Art
programme.desc: Huw Stephens explores how, from the 18th-century, the dramatic landscape of Wales captivated artists like Richard Wilson and JMW Turner, who painted it in ways that changed the course of art history. Huw also discovers the work of Penry Williams, a Merthyr Tydfil artist who was commissioned to paint his home town's vast Cyfarthfa ironworks

programme.title: Women in Film: BBC Introducing Arts
programme.desc: Cultural historian Janina Ramirez presents a collection of intriguing and exciting short films by emerging women directors and artists. Each film gives a female perspective on modern day topics from body image and new love to grief and belonging

programme.title: Life Cinematic

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: Youth activist Anita takes us on a journey to show the impact fast fashion has on our world. She is determined that fashion can be sustainable. Our ‘buy and throw away’ culture contributes to the global warming crisis so something has to be done. In a vivid animated world, we learn about the journey that many of our clothes make; from where they are manufactured, all the way to how they are transported to the UK high streets - but are there other alternatives when buying our clothes? Anita sets out to explore sustainable solutions for young people. She talks to environmentalist Zaqiya about her school initiative, Swop it Up, and Holly, manager of the UK’s largest charity shop. Together they show us ways young people can buy fashionable clothes and look after the planet at the same time.

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: Coding Genius follows 11-tear-old Emmanuella, who has a passion for coding. She hit the headlines in the UK after setting up lessons for school kids like her and is determined to go further. Now based in the Nigerian capital Abuja, Emmanuella has work to do - she wants to pass on her love of coding to other girls in the city and launches CodeKid, an after-school, weekend and holiday club for girls aged 6 – 15. But will they turn up, or will the thought of coding keep them away? In this short film, we find out what started Emmanuella’s love of coding, and get to know her extraordinary ambitions for changing the world, one coding club at a time.

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: Lydia's hair started to fall out in small patches at five years old, and she was diagnosed with the autoimmune condition alopecia areata. After years of worrying, attending various hospital appointments and using medicines that didn't improve condition significantly, she decided to shave her hair off at the age of tejn. Now she embraces her beautiful bald look, but sometimes chooses to wear a wig. With the support of her twin brother and family we take a look into her life. Lydia is determined to become a paramedic, spread awareness about alopecia and help others to love themselves, however they look.

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: Documentary following 14-year-old cheerleader and dancer Neisha as she works with disabled choreographer Kate Stanforth to devise an artistic dance video with a one-tonne industrial robot! The film combines a compelling human-interest story about how Neisha’s disability has become her strength, with first-class choreography. Neisha’s dance is one of triumph and tenderness, inspiring others to find their own strength despite the obstacles that may lie in their way.

programme.desc: Chelcea’s biggest passion is for hair! In this documentary, she shows us how she does her hair at home, doing her own hair, her mum’s hair, her sister’s hair, her friend’s hair and even her dad’s beard. Shemhas been doing lots of different hairstyles for everyone, inlcuding twists, braids (thick and small), plaits, tops buns, and also leaving it out as an afro, which is really good for the hair and scalp. Chelcea is an expert on the science of hair and how to look after it, and is particularity interested in natural hair remedies. Chelcea’s parents encourage their daughter’s passion and, through beautiful animation, we learn about her mum's childhood in the Democratic Republic of Congo. By taking us on her own hair journey, Chelcea’s determination to inspire others to celebrate their amazing hair shines through!

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters are going to Westman Islands, Iceland, where the world’s largest colony of Atlantic puffins gathers every year. Lars loves these tough little birds and explains how, although they spend most of their lives flying out to sea and fishing, they return to land each year to lay their eggs. When the baby birds, also called pufflings, eventually hatch, they fly out towards the ocean. But the light from nearby towns often confuses them about where to go, leading local children and adults to form 'puffin patrols' to rescue the pufflings. Lars is excited to help out but is upset when he discovers Glitch, also a fan of puffins, is already well-known on the islands for his skills as a puffin rescuer. Although Glitch offers him a place in his puffin patrol, Lars is determined to outdo Glitch with a patrol of his own - but when he recruits a clumsy Grimbot, things take a turn for the worse...

Safari World is opening the Arctic Dome to visitors and Mr Hammond has chosen animal costumes for Andy and Jen to wear while they show people around. Mr Hammond is worried that the walrus pups will get squished and can’t work out why there is not enough space. Andy suggests Mr Hammond goes and entertains the visitors while he and Jen work out what’s wrong. It’s time to go on an aquatic adventure! Donning his adventure gear, Andy takes to the skies in his safari-mobile, jetting off to the Arctic. Jen picks up some activity not far from the safari-mobile, so she suggests that’s a good place to start. Andy discovers that Jen

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join Stevie and Conor as they begin to discover the world of sound and see how their hearing is developed. Conor’s big brother Harrison is here to help show him around all his different musical toys, including a noisy rattle. In the garden, Stevie hangs out with mum and dad and listens to all the different sounds before getting ready to go out. Meanwhile, Conor heads over to grandma’s house while listening for sounds with his mum and brother. Stevie gets a quick hearing test at the hospital before heading home to make lots of noise with household objects. After making rainmakers at grandma’s house, Harrison and Conor get hold of a set of walkie talkies to play with. When the day is almost finished, it’s time for Stevie's parents to sing her a lullaby to help send her to sleep.

programme.title: The Toddler Club at Home

After some more refusal from the Crystal Ball, Wizard Tripwick and Gail struggle to answer the questions themselves. Keith produces his own crystal football to help his team with the answers. After saying goodbye to the Crystal Ball and totting up the final scores, the winners of this GiggleQuiz are revealed.

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos are beside a river. They watch a fisherman make his rod longer and longer and longer. The fisherman casts out the line and then settles down for a nap. Chickedy and Chick run along the rod to the very end. Toodloo runs along the rod and jumps to join Chickedy and Chick. This causes the rod to spring up sending them flying! The fisherman wakes to discover a long, long, long fish at the end of his line. The Twirlywoo Screen draws a small line that Chickedy and Chick help make longer. Toodloo helps the line get a little longer still but when Great BigHoo taps the screen, the line becomes so long it shoots off the Twirlywoo Screen. The line gets longer and longer and goes into the Big Red Boat - right into Peekaboo's house! The Twirlywoos run after the Twirly Rings whilst Peekaboo reels in the long line

After breakfast, Iffah and her brother Walli play together - he does a drawing while she plays with her toy duck. Meanwhile, Myles and his dad heads to a local park, where they look at the lake and see lots of different birds. Iffah’s family go to an indoor play area, where Iffah has fun playing in the ball pool. When Myles and his dad arrive at great grandma’s house, Myles reads a book with grandma and even turns the pages all on his own! After they join up with mum, they all visit a quarry to feed some fish. Iffah finishes the day with her family at a petting farm, where she gets to stroke an alpaca.

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. There is a turtle expert visiting the Safari World aquarium. Mr Hammond is on a scuba diving holiday in Borneo, so it’s up to Andy to ensure everything is ready for their arrival. Andy does a great job making the aquarium looking its best, but when Mr Hammond asks Andy if he has cleaned the green sea turtles, Andy is a little confused as he thought they cleaned themselves. Mr Hammond informs Jen and Andy that green sea turtles get clean by letting little fish nibble away the dirt and parasites on their skin and shell. However, he neglects to tell them what kind of fish, so Andy decides to find out for himself. It’s time to go on an aquatic adventure! Andy blasts off through a magic portal in his safari-sub and heads to a beautiful coral reef off the coast of Bor
